text: The female athlete triad involves interrelationships among energy availability, menstrual function, and bone mineral density, with clinical manifestations including eating disorders, functional hypothalamic amenorrhea, and osteoporosis.
Low energy availability is the primary factor that impairs both reproductive and skeletal health in the female athlete triad.
Most pathological effects of the female athlete triad appear to occur below an energy availability of 30 kcal per kg of fat-free mass per day.
The first aim of treatment for any female athlete triad component is to increase energy availability by increasing energy intake and/or reducing exercise energy expenditure.
No pharmacological agent adequately restores bone loss or corrects metabolic abnormalities that impair health and performance in athletes with functional hypothalamic amenorrhea.
population: female athletes with functional hypothalamic amenorrhea

text: Female athletes in sports or physical activities that emphasize leanness are at special risk for restrictive eating behaviors and the female athlete triad.
Athletes should be screened for the female athlete triad at the preparticipation physical and whenever they present with any of the triad's clinical conditions.
